steps.label: cause
steps.text: Warm growing environment (high temperatures, low-latitude, or continental interior location)

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Rapid ripening accelerates sugar accumulation in the grape berry; grapes reach high sugar levels before other components (acidity, tannin) have time to fully develop

steps.label: effect
steps.text: High sugar content at harvest ferments to high alcohol levels; wines tend to show lower acidity, fuller body, and richer fruit flavour

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Warm climates accelerate ripening, increasing sugar and thus potential alcohol, producing fuller-bodied wines with lower acidity.

sat_relevance: Use when assessing high alcohol as a structural feature in warm-climate wines. High alcohol contributes to body and texture but can appear unbalanced if fruit concentration or acidity is insufficient.
